title: LAC Ecosystem Overview
problem: Visitors who understand what life-as-code is still don't know what the ecosystem looks like in practice — what tools exist, how they relate to each other, and which one to reach for. Without a map of the packages and apps, the workflow feels like an abstract idea rather than something they can actually adopt.
analysis: The LAC ecosystem has three deployment surfaces: the CLI (npm), the VS Code extension (Marketplace), and the web app (Vercel). Under the hood, several internal packages power them — the schema, the LSP, the MCP server, and the Claude integration. Visitors don't need to understand all of them to get started, but they do need a map that shows the entry points and how the pieces connect. Each package gets its own child feature with its own decisions and context — this overview is the index that ties them together.

decisions.decision: One child feature per package/app, this feature as their shared parent
decisions.rationale: Each package has its own problem, decisions, and tradeoffs that deserve independent documentation. The ecosystem overview is the index — it explains the shape of the map; the children fill in the territory.
alternativesConsidered: One long feature with all packages — loses the per-package decision context, hard to navigate
No overview, just individual package features — visitors have no entry point to understand how the pieces relate

decisions.decision: Ecosystem overview frozen once all 7 package children are complete and rendering in the tree
decisions.rationale: All package features (007-013) are filled, frozen, and visible as an indented sub-tree. The thread-toggle on this card lets visitors collapse the entire package detail in one click.

lineage.spawnReason: The what-is-LAC explainer introduces the concept; the ecosystem overview is the natural next step — here's what you can actually install and use.

knownLimitations: Overview becomes stale whenever a new package is added and a child feature isn't spawned to match
successCriteria: The ecosystem overview renders in the feature tree as a parent of all individual package features. A visitor reading top-to-bottom can follow the LAC concept → ecosystem map → individual package deep-dives.
implementation: feat-2026-006 is a content-only feature — no dedicated UI component. It renders as a feature card in the tree at depth 2 (child of feat-2026-004, grandchild of feat-2026-001). Its 7 child features (feat-2026-007 through 013) appear indented beneath it at depth 3, each with their own domain badge, tags, and full expanded content. The thread-toggle on this card collapses all 7 package cards in one click, letting visitors skip the ecosystem detail if they only care about the high-level story. The feature serves as the index node — its analysis field describes the map; the children fill in the territory.
